Title: A phytoplasma effector destabilizes chloroplastic glutamine synthetase inducing chlorotic leaves that attract leafhopper vectors
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chlorophyll supports photosynthesis and leaf green coloration; glutamine/glutamate/5-ALA serve as precursors for chlorophyll biosynthesis and nitrogen assimilation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Phytoplasma Sec-dependent secreted effector with N-terminal signal peptide
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted from phloem-localized phytoplasma; translocated to chloroplasts of mesophyll cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in ROLP-infected rice tissues; transcript and protein levels increased in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic rice, constitutive expression caused chlorotic leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: constitutive overexpression in transgenic rice; specific promoter not stated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in rice; subcellular localization; Y2H; GST pull-down; co-IP; BiFC; chloroplast purification; metabolite profiling; native PAGE holoenzyme assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes leaf yellowing/chlorosis by reducing GS2 activity and chlorophyll precursor accumulation, thereby increasing attractiveness to leafhopper vectors and facilitating phytoplasma transmission
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SRP1 is the only tested ROLP secreted candidate effector that localized to chloroplasts. 2. SRP1 directly interacts with OsGS2 and requires residue E52 for this interaction and chloroplast targeting. 3. SRP1 disrupts OsGS2 decamer formation, suppresses GS2 activity, decreases Gln, Glu and 5-ALA, reduces chlorophyll, and induces yellow leaves. 4. SRP1 transgenic rice attracts more leafhoppers and supports higher ROLP accumulation after inoculation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence-based effector screening; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ROLP infection; leafhopper feeding; glutamine supplementation (0.5 g/L) partially rescued yellowing and insect attraction
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Leaf chlorosis, slender growth phenotype, reduced photosynthetic parameters
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering/vector control by blocking SRP1 delivery or SRP1-OsGS2 interaction; caution because trait is pathogen virulence-associated
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SRP1 is an effector/regulator of host metabolism, it is not a transcription factor or enzyme, so classified as Common Gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen assimilation and provision of precursors for chlorophyll biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chloroplastic glutamine synthetase 2, catalytic glutamine synthetase domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rice leaves; mRNA and protein reduced upon ROLP infection and in SRP1 transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf chloroplast-associated; endogenous decamer abundant in healthy leaves
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native endogenous expression; transient overexpression in N. benthamiana not promoter-specified
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; deletion mapping; GST pull-down; co-IP; BiFC; enzyme activity assay; native PAGE; metabolite profiling; infection analysis; transgenic SRP1 perturbation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive determinant of glutamine/glutamate and chlorophyll precursor production; inhibition or destabilization decreases chlorophyll and causes chlorosis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS2 directly binds SRP1 through its C-terminal catalytic domain. 2. OsGS2 localizes to chloroplasts and recruits SRP1 into chloroplasts. 3. OsGS2 predominantly forms a decameric holoenzyme in rice; SRP1 disrupts this oligomer and inhibits activity. 4. Reduced OsGS2 activity correlates with increased NH4+ and decreased Gln, Glu, 5-ALA and chlorophyll in infected or SRP1-expressing plants.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ROLP infection; in vitro addition of purified SRP1; glutamine supplementation rescue context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower activity associated with chlorosis, reduced amino acid levels, reduced photosynthetic parameters, slender growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering disease tolerance by preserving GS2 holoenzyme stability or preventing effector binding; caution due to central role in nitrogen assimilation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core metabolic gene but functions enzymatically in nitrogen assimilation; classified as Pathway Gene rather than Common in this study context

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase family; chloroplastic GS2 catalytic domain
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nitrogen assimilation linked to chlorophyll biosynthesis precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Chloroplastic ammonium assimilation / precursor supply for tetrapyrrole-chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent assimilation of ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for chlorophyll biosynthesis through glutamine/glutamate and 5-ALA formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for chlorophyll precursor supply; supported as decisive control point in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in rice leaves; transcript and protein levels decline during ROLP infection and in SRP1 transgenic plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly active in healthy green leaves; endogenous protein mainly exists as decamer
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native endogenous expression; transient agroexpression constructs in N. benthamiana, promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme activity assay; metabolite quantification; Y2H; pull-down; co-IP; BiFC; native PAGE oligomer assay; chloroplast localization; infection and transgenic assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher/stable OsGS2 promotes chlorophyll precursor synthesis and green leaves; SRP1-mediated inhibition lowers Gln/Glu/5-ALA and causes chlorosis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS2 is the rice chloroplastic target of the phytoplasma effector SRP1. 2. OsGS2 likely forms a decameric holoenzyme required for full activity. 3. SRP1 binding disrupts OsGS2 oligomerization without directly degrading monomer protein in short-term assays, suppressing enzymatic activity. 4. Reduced OsGS2 function is associated with increased ammonium and decreased Gln, Glu, 5-ALA, and chlorophyll, causing yellow leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ROLP infection; transient coexpression with SRP1 in N. benthamiana; purified SRP1 addition in vitro; glutamine supplementation rescue
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption leads to chlorosis, reduced photosynthesis, lower total amino acid content, and growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for editing effector-binding interface while preserving catalytic function to maintain chlorophyll and reduce pathogen-enhanced vector attraction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper links OsGS2 to chlorophyll indirectly via precursor supply rather than direct chlorophyll enzymology.
